item.title: Entromics
item.description: Genome sequence contains information, which - when used as input into exact thermodynamic formula - is recalculated into energy, needed to incorporate variant base into any specific position in the DNA strand. This lead to discovery of coherence for these incorporation entropies along the complete genome sequence. Short explanation of this phenomenon and how it is quantitatively characterized can be found here. For more details see our paper “Entromics – thermodynamics of sequence dependent base incorporation into DNA reveals novel long-distance genome organization”.

item.title: Personalized Network Medicine (PNM)
item.description:
Representative results for predicting complications after surgical stent insertion by Personalized Network Medicine processing of electronic medical records.

PNM provides individual risk ratio of post-procedure complications and quantitative model for “What if” simulation of personal, environmental and clinical factors impact on the complication risk.
